title: 对这一代心力交瘁的年轻人而言，麻将已成为当下最受欢迎的第三空间
content: Mahjong has become the leading third‑place for today’s exhausted young people, offering a social and relaxing outlet beyond home and work. It provides a communal, low‑pressure environment where they can unwind, connect with friends, and temporarily escape the demands of modern life. This trend highlights how casual, culturally familiar activities are filling the gap for informal social spaces.

Last night with friends in Shanghai I noted that the current AI revolution will generate far more wealth than the previous internet era, creating many new ultra‑high‑net‑worth individuals. Over 500 companies in Hong Kong, about a quarter AI‑related, are lining up for IPOs, while U.S. and A‑share markets also drive the trend. U.S. exchanges set AI prices and host future unicorns like OpenAI and SpaceX. Hong Kong attracts Chinese AI firms with new listing rules, while the A‑share market anchors technology into domestic manufacturing. This three‑market synergy will accelerate wealth creation beyond previous digital cycles.
source: 热帖 - 雪球 - 但斌

title: DeepSeek给AI装了根赛博手指，于是它能看见了
content: DeepSeek released a multimodal vision report that shifts focus from high‑resolution perception to stable reference in reasoning. It introduces “visual primitives”—bounding boxes and points—as core neural symbols, letting the model point to objects while reasoning (“write and point”). This approach, called “point‑while‑reasoning,” addresses the “reference gap” that models face when language alone cannot disambiguate space. The system also uses compressed sparse attention to sharply reduce token counts (≈90 vs 1000+), boosting inference speed, memory use, and scalability.

title: China Travel 2.0：不只是旅游，更是万亿服贸出海
content: China’s inbound tourism has shifted from tourism‑only to a multi‑layered “China Travel 2.0” model, generating new service‑trade revenue. Tourists now seek technology tours, local culinary experiences, medical care, and lower‑tier city visits; agencies and hotels adapt by offering multilingual services, digital payments, and customized itineraries. Entrepreneurs build niche services—tech‑theme tours, cross‑border payment platforms, and local‑experience agencies—to capture this burgeoning market, which can boost GDP, the RMB, and China’s global image.

He warns that lingering in academia out of habit can waste both personal and scientific progress.

content: The article examines Guangzhou cinema manager Li Weiming’s strategy for the 2026 May Day movie lineup, which features 17 films—more than last year—with diverse genres but modest pre‑sales and modest box‑office totals (≈68 m RMB). Without a clear blockbuster lead, scheduling is now data‑driven, adjusting daily to protect profits. Industry insiders discuss the challenge of regaining audience traffic from short‑form video; suggestions include classic re‑releases, targeted marketing, and hybrid “mini‑theatre” experiences. The piece highlights how the May Day window, while key to yearly revenue, is under pressure from declining visitor counts and rising competition.

content: The Hacker News roundup reports several tech incidents: a Linux kernel privilege‑escalation flaw (CVE‑2026‑31431) that exploits AF_ALG and splice to modify setuid files and elevate local users; a Claude Code billing bug that mis‑charges for commits containing “HERMES.md”; OpenAI’s removal of “goblin” metaphors from Codex after discovering they were over‑used; a “OpenClaw” keyword triggering Claude Code rejection or extra billing; Belgium halting nuclear plant decommissioning in favor of state ownership; the Zig project banning LLM‑assisted contributions to avoid low‑quality PRs; Mozilla opposing Google’s Prompt API over privacy concerns; Meta ending a contract with an outsourcing firm after employees exposed privacy violations in smart‑glasses content review; Meta’s handling of an NSA surveillance exposé by Mark Klein; and Spain’s parliament moving to limit IP blockages imposed by LaLiga for anti‑piracy.

title: 职场残酷预演：当AI学会自动进化，初级人才的生存空间正被“200小时智能体”挤压殆尽
content: A simulation at METR examined how a 200‑hour AI could reshape work. Participants experienced 3‑to‑5‑fold efficiency gains, but new bottlenecks emerged in prioritization, feedback loops, and project management. As AI handles routine tasks instantly, human judgment, iteration, and validation become the decision‑making edge. The exercise predicts a future where AI drives execution, while human expertise must focus on direction, oversight, and ensuring correctness.

title: 白宫反对 Anthropic 扩大 Mythos 模型使用范围的计划
content: The White House opposes Anthropic’s plan to broaden access to its AI model Mythos, which can launch cyberattacks and create widespread network disruption. Anthropic would allow around 70 additional companies and organizations, raising the total number of authorized users to about 120. U.S. officials cited safety concerns and a lack of sufficient computing power that could limit the government’s ability to use the model.

title: 马斯克诉 OpenAI 案法庭证词与推文矛盾，称特斯拉未研发通用人工智能
content: Elon Musk appeared in California federal court to sue Sam Altman and other OpenAI founders, alleging they “stole from a nonprofit” and misrepresented the company’s transition to for‑profit status. Musk claimed that switches in Microsoft’s investment restrictions prompted the lawsuit and insisted that revenue limits fundamentally differ for investors. He also contradicted his own statements about Tesla’s AI projects and faced questions over hiring OpenAI staff. The judge paused some questioning, allowing limited testimony before the hearing continues on Thursday.

title: OpenAI 硬件负责人的闭门分享：硬件的「终点」依旧是智能手机
content: OpenAI is moving into hardware to secure control over AI’s low‑level operations. The company has already tape‑out a chip in two years and plans a smartphone that meets future model demands, rather than just building an agent. By hiring former Jony Ive team members, OpenAI aims to design end‑side devices. Unlike other AI firms, it seeks a full‑stack redefinition, shifting from model provider to infrastructure and device company, with the phone as a natural step toward an agent‑centric computing paradigm.

title: 小红书组织调整：任命柯南为总裁，加大 AI 投入

Concurrently, Conan was appointed president to unify the community, e‑commerce, and commercialization units, reporting directly to CEO Seiya, while Mulan remains chair of the board.

title: 无人机实名登记和激活要求将于 5 月 1 日起全国实施
content: China’s Civil Aviation Administration introduced a mandatory national standard on May 1 requiring all civil unmanned aircraft operating outdoors to be registered and activated with real‑name credentials. The rule establishes a traceable management system designed to protect public safety, maintain airspace order, and safeguard personal rights. It applies equally to all qualifying operators, ensuring consistent enforcement across the country.

title: Shai-Hulud Themed Malware Found in the PyTorch Lightning AI Training Library
content: Researchers identified malware designed on a Shai‑Hulud theme embedded in the PyTorch Lightning AI training library. The malicious code exploits the library’s distribution mechanism to deliver a cryptojacker that hijacks user resources, compromising systems that install or update the library. The discovery highlights the need for stricter oversight of open‑source AI frameworks and confirms that seemingly legitimate packages can be weaponized for covert attacks.

content: Research shows that extending the time between study sessions by tenfold while reducing the amount of practice to one‑tenth can achieve the same learning outcomes as continuous study, with the same total time spent. This effect is driven by the brain’s automatic adaptive mechanisms rather than willpower.

title: 经常午睡，中风风险涨24%？最新研究：每周午睡1-2次，每次30分钟，心血管疾病风险降近50%；且老年人减少午睡，认知衰退显著延 | 原文
content: Recent research shows that frequent afternoon naps can raise stroke risk by 24 %. However, limiting naps to 1–2 times per week for about 30 minutes each reduces cardiovascular disease risk by nearly 50 %. Additionally, older adults who cut down on napping experience a notable delay in cognitive decline. The study highlights a trade‑off between the restorative benefits of short naps and potential long‑term health risks.

title: GitHub Copilot CLI for Beginners: Interactive v. non-interactive mode
content: GitHub Copilot CLI offers two modes: interactive and non‑interactive. Interactive mode, activated by simply typing `copilot`, provides a chat‑like session where you can ask questions, review answers, and continue the dialogue within a single session. Non‑interactive mode, invoked with `copilot -p "prompt"`, delivers a quick, single response directly in the shell, ideal for one‑off tasks like summarizing code or generating snippets. Sessions can be resumed with `/resume` or `copilot --resume`.
